title: Group X - Total Body Strength and Conditioning
description:
Instructor-led strength and conditioning class designed to hit every major muscle group in just 30 minutes. Using a combination of compound lifts, bodyweight movements, and targeted conditioning work, each session is programmed to build real strength and leave you energized for the rest of the day. Instructors scale every movement so you work at the right intensity for your level. 
 How do I Sign-up? 
 
 Registration is not required. 
 FREE for students with a valid Montclair State ID 
 
 Where and When? 
 
 Tuesdays , July 6 - July 30 
 12:00 pm - 12:30 pm 
 Student Recreation Center - Multipurpose Room 1 
 
 Instructor: Jeffrey

title: Red Hawk Pantry
description:
1) Please have your Event Pass, Student ID, CWID and have a  PantrySoft Registration .  2) Please bring  your own shopping bag ! ***Visitors are allowed to come once/week*** 
 Our Mission:   We aim to address food insecurity across Montclair State University. The Red Hawk Pantry is open to all students, alumni, staff, and faculty at no cost.  Stay updated by following us on Instagram:  @montclair_redhawkpantry 
 🌻 Summer 2026 Hours 
 
 Wednesdays: 12-2pm 
 
 If you are unable to visit during our regular hours, please reach out via email at redhawkpantry@montclair.edu

title: Prestigious Scholarships Info Session
description: Join our weekly Prestigious Scholarships Info Sessions to learn about nationally competitive scholarships and fellowships that support graduate study, research, internships, public service, and international opportunities. Learn more about awards such as Marshall Rhodes, Truman, and more, while exploring eligibility, application timelines, and program support at  Montclair State University . Open to students from all majors. Sessions are held Wednesdays at 1:00 PM on Zoom. RSVP on Engage to receive the Zoom link. A google calendar invite will be sent once RSVP.

title: Camping and Surfing Trip
location: Meet at the Student Recreation Center
description:
July 7th, 8th and 9th 
 $30 Student; $75 Faculty/Staff 
 Join Campus Recreation for a new and exciting trip where we will camp in the beautiful Wharton State Forest and surf on the beaches of Ocean City, NJ! We will head to Wharton State Forest for a relaxing night of camping. The next day we will head to Ocean City to enjoy the sun and waves. We return to the campsite that evening, and depart Thursday morning to come back to campus. We provide all of the equipment and transportation, including tents, sleeping bags, ground pads, surfing lessons and surfboards, plus beach badges for the afternoon! Meals are included. 
     How do I Sign-up? 
 
 Register online through Fusion, in the Student Rec Center main office, or over the phone (973-655-3340). 
 Cost for Students $30; Faculty/Staff $75 
 
   Where and When? 
 
 July 7th, 8th and 9th, 2026 
 July 7th 10 am- July 9th 5pm  
 Begins at Student Recreation Center 
 
 What to bring:  Water bottle, bug spray, sunscreen, towel, bathing suit, clothes for 2 nights and 3 days, toiletries, chargers, bag for wet clothing, sneakers, money (optional for boardwalk) 
 Potential Hazards:  Unpredictable weather changes, sunburn/heat exhaustion, dehydration, allergic reactions/poisonous plants, insect bites, falls from a height, abrasions from sand, uncontrolled interactions with wildlife, drowning from rough surf.

description:
As part of the Summer Bridge Program, Intramural Sports is excited to offer Open Play Volleyball This program provides prospective college students with an opportunity to stay active, build connections with their peers, and experience campus recreation in a fun and welcoming environment. title: Hike - Ramapo Mountain
location: Meet at the Student Recreation Center
description:
This trail features a loop around the beautiful Ramapo Lake into a bit of steep climb to the ruins of the Van Slyke Castle and water tower. From the water tower, there is a view gorgeous view of NYC. 
 How do I Sign-up? 
 
 Register online through Fusion, in the Student Recreation Center's main office, or over the phone (973-655-3340). 
 Cost for Students  $10 ; Faculty/Staff $20 
 
 Where and when? 
 
 Thursday, July 16th, 2026 
 10:00am – 5:00pm 
 Begins at Student Recreation Center 
 
 What to bring:  Water, lunch, weather appropriate clothing, boots/ sneakers ,  lunch, snacks, and sunscreen.

title: Whitewater Rafting Trip
location: Meet at the Student Recreation Center
description:
Coordinating with the release of the dam in the Lehigh River, this whitewater rafting trip is sure to be a blast. Perfect for all skill levels. This is a great way to GET YOUR FEET WET!  
   
 How do I Sign-up? 
 
 Register online through Fusion, in the Student Recreation Center's main office, or over the phone (973-655-3340). 
 Cost for Students  $20 ; Faculty/Staff $60 
 
   
 Where and when? 
 
 Saturday, July 25th, 2026 
 7:00am – 7:00pm 
 Begins at Student Recreation Center 
 
   
 What to bring:  Lunch, water, snacks, towel, a change of clothes and sandals with a heel strap, old sneakers, or water shoes. Flip-flops or slides are not allowed. For warmer days, wear a swim suit or quick-drying shorts/shirt; for cooler days, bring extra layers, but no cotton. Wetsuits are included for those that want them. Half/full wetsuits can be rented from the organization. Half is $6, full is $12, jacket is $4.  
   
 Potential Hazards:  Unpredictable weather changes, sunburn/heat exhaustion, limited vision, dehydration, allergic reactions/poisonous plants, insect bites and injury by running, including sprains, lacerations, abrasions and fractures.

title: Going Global: A Free Two-Part Workshop Series
organization: Feliciano Center for Entrepreneurship and Innovation
description: Ready to take your business beyond U.S. borders? Join us for a FREE two-part workshop series designed to transform your growth from local to worldwide. On  August 4 , we'll explore whether exporting is the right move for your business, assess your readiness, and identify which of your products or services have international demand. By  August 18 , you'll be ready for part two, where we'll dive into the real logistics—finding international buyers, navigating permits and paperwork, and mapping out your clear path forward. Expert facilitator Reina Valenzuela will guide you through the opportunities, close your knowledge gaps, and leave you with a concrete action plan. Whether you're curious about global expansion or ready to take the leap, this free series gives you the framework and clarity to do it right.

title: Prestigious Scholarships Info Session
description: Join our weekly Prestigious Scholarships Info Sessions to learn about nationally competitive scholarships and fellowships that support graduate study, research, internships, public service, and international opportunities. Learn more about awards such as Marshall Rhodes, Truman, and more, while exploring eligibility, application timelines, and program support at  Montclair State University . Open to students from all majors. Sessions are held Wednesdays at 1:00 PM on Zoom. RSVP on Engage to receive the Zoom link. A google calendar invite will be sent once RSVP.

title: Free Summer Membership for Faculty/Staff
description:
Free summer memberships at the Student Recreation Center are back and better than ever! This year, we've extended the program to three full months (May 7 – August 7) and have newly upgraded fitness spaces and equipment for you to enjoy. Membership provides unlimited access to our state-of-the-art facilities, indoor heated pool, and more. No registration required, simply bring your employee ID to access the building all summer long. 
 
 
 
 
 How do I sign-up? 
 
 Registration not required 
 For employees of Montclair/Bloomfield 
 
 Where and when? 
 
 Whenever SRC is open, from  May 7th - August 7th  
 Student Recreation Center

description: Join our weekly Prestigious Scholarships Info Sessions to learn about nationally competitive scholarships and fellowships that support graduate study, research, internships, public service, and international opportunities. Learn more about awards such as Marshall Rhodes, Truman, and more, while exploring eligibility, application timelines, and program support at  Montclair State University . title: Going Global: A Free Two-Part Workshop Series
organization: Feliciano Center for Entrepreneurship and Innovation
description: Ready to take your business beyond U.S. borders? Join us for a FREE two-part workshop series designed to transform your growth from local to worldwide. On  August 4 , we'll explore whether exporting is the right move for your business, assess your readiness, and identify which of your products or services have international demand. By  August 18 , you'll be ready for part two, where we'll dive into the real logistics—finding international buyers, navigating permits and paperwork, and mapping out your clear path forward. Expert facilitator Reina Valenzuela will guide you through the opportunities, close your knowledge gaps, and leave you with a concrete action plan. Whether you're curious about global expansion or ready to take the leap, this free series gives you the framework and clarity to do it right.

title: Build Your Own Self Care Kits
description:
Build Your Own Self-Care Kit with Advocacy & Outreach 
 Take a moment to invest in your well-being! Join Advocacy & Outreach to create your own personalized self-care kit filled with items to help you manage stress, practice mindfulness, and prioritize your mental and emotional wellness throughout the semester. While you build your kit, meet our team, learn about the resources and support available through Advocacy & Outreach, and discover ways to stay connected on campus. Stop by, get creative, and leave with tools to help you thrive all year long!

title: Tropical Bash
description: Get ready to kick off the school year with our annual Tropical Bash! Join us for an unforgettable outdoor event, where the warm vibes of the tropics meet the excitement of a new academic year. This event is the perfect way to reconnect and relax before starting the new semester. You won’t want to miss it! Free smoothies, DJ, 360 photobooth, and more!

title: Sunset Mountain Top Yoga
location: Meet at the Panzer Athletic Center
description:
Join us for a peaceful hike to the mountaintop where we’ll unwind with a relaxing yoga session surrounded by beautiful views and fresh air. After the hike back down, end the night by the fire with s’mores, bingo, and great company. It’s the perfect mix of adventure, relaxation, and fun! 
   
 How do I Sign-up? 
 
 Please register in the Student Recreation Center main office or over the phone (973-655-3340). 
 
 Where and When? 
 
 Friday the 28th, 2026 
 6:30pm-8:30pm 
 Start Place is Panzer 
 
   
 Potential Hazards: Unpredictable weather changes, sunburn/heat exhaustion, limited vision, dehydration, allergic reactions/poisonous plants, insect bites and injury by running, including sprains, lacerations, abrasions and fractures.

title: Welcome Back Bingo
description: Join Campus Activities for Bingo at the Amphitheater. Grab a seat, grab a card, and get ready to shout  BINGO! to  win a prize basket! There will be over $500 in prizes!!

title: Late Night Cereal Bar
description: Who says cereal is just for breakfast? Join Campus Activities for a  Late-Night Cereal Bar  in the Student Center Cafeteria! Build your dream bowl with a wide variety of cereals, milks, and sweet toppings — the perfect study break or late-night snack.

title: Deck Out Your Dorm Day
description: Ready to make your dorm feel like  you ? Come hang out and personalize your space at  Deck Out Your Dorm Day ! We’ve got everything you need — design your own pennant, tie-dye a pillowcase, create custom coasters or picture frames, and make some fun name art for your door.

 Attend and receive a raffle ticket for a chance to win a prize!

description:
Kick off your college experience by joining Advocacy & Outreach for Queer Trivia Night! 
   
 Test your knowledge of LGBTQ+ pop culture, music, movies, television, history, and iconic trailblazers while connecting with fellow students in a fun and welcoming environment. Whether you're a longtime fan of queer pop culture or just looking to meet new people, this event is a great way to build community, celebrate LGBTQ+ identities and experiences, and discover resources that support belonging on campus. 
   
 Come for the trivia, stay for the connections, and compete for exciting prizes!

description:
Ready to take your education global? Join us at the Study Abroad Fair and explore a world of opportunities waiting just beyond your campus. Discover programs around the world, meet representatives from our partners and providers, and hear firsthand stories from students who’ve already taken the leap. 
 Whether you're dreaming of practicing your language skills, expanding your horizons in a new corner of the globe, or even seeing a different part of the United States, this is your chance to find the perfect fit. Learn about scholarships, internships, and how to make your journey abroad both affordable and unforgettable.

title: Kayaking on the Delaware River
description:
Enjoy a relaxing day paddling down the Delaware River and learn some basic kayaking skills. Leaving from Montclair State University, NJ, this full-day trip will provide you with a chance to enjoy the warm, clear waters of the longest undammed river east of the Mississippi. We provide the boats, life jackets and paddles, but be prepared to get wet. 
   How do I Sign-up? 
 
 Register online through Fusion, in the Student Rec Center main office, or over the phone (973-655-3340). 
 Cost for Students $10;  $20  Faculty/Staff 
 
 Where and When? 
 
 September 19th, 2026 
 9:00 am – 6:00 pm    
 Begins at Student Recreation Center 
 
 What to bring:  Water, lunch, bathing suit, towel, sunblock and a change of clothes. 
   
 Potential Hazards:  under/above water obstructions, rapidly moving water, poisonous plants/allergies, insect bites, unpredictable weather changes, sunburn/heat exhaustion, dehydration

title: AEW: FIND YOUR CENTER 2026
location: Student Center Ballroom A / The Quad
description:
The Discovery Program is just one of the many different departments on campus committed to your success at Montclair State University. 
 Join us and our campus partners for an engaging and centering experience to help you explore all that Montclair has to offer. Use the clues at check-in to help you figure out what each center has to offer you.   Come have fun, make connections, enjoy some refreshments and snacks, and learn about the various centers on campus! 
 Find Your Center is part of  Academic Exploration Week , a longstanding immersive experience presented by  The Discovery Program . To contact the Discovery Program, email us at  discoveryprogram@montclair.edu , or visit us at  montclair.edu/discovery-program .

title: AEW: MAJOR KICKOFF 2026
location: The Student Center Ballrooms ABC + Patio / The Quad + Flagpoles
description:
Major Kickoff a high-energy, campus-wide event that transforms a traditional majors fair into an interactive and dynamic experience that sets the tone for major exploration.  Major Kickoff  serves as a central hub where exploratory ("undeclared") students can dive into the wide array of majors offered at Montclair in a fun and engaging way. Students will engage with peers, faculty, and staff; discover pathways to get involved in various programs; and gain the guidance they need to make confident, informed decisions about their major. With the opportunity to declare a major on the spot,  Major Kickoff  empowers students to take charge of their academic journey. 
 With over 50 departments, programs, clubs, and more on site, you never know what you might discover or who you might meet.  Enjoy free food, games, music, and giveaways, win prizes and T-shirts , and maybe even discover your major. 
 Major Kickoff is part of Academic Exploration Week, a longstanding immersive experience presented by The Discovery Program. To contact the Discovery Program, email us at  discoveryprogram@montclair.edu , or visit us at  montclair.edu/discovery-program .

title: Patriotic Lego Trivia (Montclair Campus)
description:
Think you know your U.S. history, government, and pop culture? Put your knowledge to the test in our high-energy game of  Patriotic Trivia !💥 Join us for a fun and fast-paced showdown where teams will compete in classic Jeopardy style, answering trivia questions that celebrate all things America—from the Constitution and civic milestones to iconic moments and figures that shaped our nation.  Bring your A-game and your appetite—yes, there’s free food, because life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness tastes better on a full stomach. 
 Open to all students —bring your love for democracy, and maybe even a little friendly competition. Prizes, bragging rights, and patriotic pride are on the line. Let’s see who really knows their stars and stripes!

title: A&O Open House
description:
Calling all Red Hawks! 
 Join Advocacy & Outreach on Wednesday, September 23, 2026 from 2-4:00 PM in our office suite (Student Center Suite 113) for our annual Open House! 
 Bring your friends and learn all about our community-based programs, our 2 resource centers, what we offer via workshops, training, discussion groups, events, and soooo much more! 
 You'll be able to meet the professional, graduate, and student staff of A&O, have fun by participating in arts activities, photobooths, socialize with new folk, eat free food, and win prizes! 
 You heard right ... we have games, free food, AND prizes! You do not want to miss this!

title: AEW: SKILLS IN ACTION 2026
description:
Wondering how your strengths can shape your future? This interactive workshop will help you discover and build on the skills that matter most to  you .  In this session, you’ll: 
 Identify your skills and strengths through self-assessment. 
 Determine how those skills connect to possible majors and career paths. 
 Create an action plan to grow your skills using the Discovery Program's AI-powered  Major Mentor . Additionally, Major Mentor will help you map majors that align with your strengths and provide  real next steps . 
 Skills in Action: Mapping Your Strengths to Majors  is part of  Academic Exploration Week , a longstanding immersive experience presented by  The Discovery Program . To contact the Discovery Program, email us at  discoveryprogram@montclair.edu , or visit us at  montclair.edu/discovery-program .

title: Tote Bag Decorating
description: Join Campus Activities for Craft Night! Design your own tote bag with fun colors, patterns, and creative flair.

title: AEW: MAJOR MYSTERY 2026
description:
Major Mystery : Can You Crack the Case? Strange clues… puzzling paths… and majors shrouded in mystery. Gather your team, study the evidence, and connect the right majors to the right careers before time runs out! Solve career “cases” by piecing together path cards filled with cryptic clues. The more connections you crack, the more points you earn. Will your group uncover the truth… or will the mystery remain unsolved? 
 Major Mystery is part of  Academic Exploration Week , a longstanding immersive experience presented by  The Discovery Program . To contact the Discovery Program, email us at  discoveryprogram@montclair.edu , or visit us at  montclair.edu/discovery-program .

title: CCOM Alumni Panel
organization: The Office for Experiential Education and Career Connections
location: Presentation Hall - College of Communication and Media
description:
Join alumni from the  College of Communication and Media  as they share their career journeys, discuss how they transitioned from college to the professional world, and offer advice for students interested in creative careers. 
 Panelists will discuss : 
 
 Building a career in the communication and media industries 
 Navigating internships, auditions, and professional opportunities 
 Developing industry connections and professional networks 
 Skills that helped them succeed after graduation 
 Challenges, opportunities, and lessons learned along the way 
 
 Featured career areas include : 
 
 Assistant Account Executive 
 Co-Founder & Creative Director 
 Social Video Producer 
 Video Editor & Productions 
 
 Students will have the opportunity to ask questions, learn about different career paths, and gain firsthand insights from CCOM alumni who have successfully launched their careers. 
   
 Register on Handshake here!

title: Pride and Progress: Celebrating LGBTQ+ and Disability Journeys
description:
Join Advocacy and Outreach for our 3rd Annual LGBTQ+ and Disability History and Awareness Month Flag Raising and Reception — a joyful celebration of identity, resilience, and community! 
 Pride and Progress brings together students, faculty, and staff to honor the rich histories and vibrant futures of LGBTQ+ and disability communities. Enjoy an inspiring flag-raising ceremony featuring an energetic MC, powerful speakers, and a special live performance you won’t want to miss. 
 After the ceremony, the celebration continues with: - Music & vibes - Delicious food - A hands-on craft activity - A photo activation station - Free LGBTQ+ and Disability Pride flags - Interactive tabling from campus partners 
 This event is about visibility, solidarity, and celebration — open to everyone, including allies! Come as you are, bring your friends, and help us raise our voices (and our flags!) in honor of progress, pride, and community power.

title: Skills for Success: The Truth About Soft Skills
description: Think success in college and beyond is all about technical know-how? Think again! This interactive workshop uncovers the real power of soft skills—like communication, teamwork, problem-solving, conflict resolution, adaptability, and discipline—that employers and professors alike value most. Through hands-on activities and real-world examples, you’ll learn how to recognize, strengthen, and showcase these essential skills to thrive as a student and future professional. This workshop is presented by  The Discovery Program . To contact the Discovery Program, email us at  discoveryprogram@montclair.edu , or visit us at  montclair.edu/discovery-program .

title: Design Your Day: Discover Your Direction
description:
Not sure what to major in or where you're headed after college? Let’s change that—starting with the kind of  day  you want to have. In this hands-on, creative workshop, you’ll: 
 1) Visualize your ideal workday—where you are, who you’re with, and what you’re doing. 
 2) Identify your key work-life preferences, like flexibility, collaboration, or creativity. 
 3) Explore majors and careers that align with your vision through DP’s AI-powered  Major Mentor , a tool that matches your preferences to Montclair majors and career paths. 
 This workshop is presented by  The Discovery Program . To contact the Discovery Program, email us at  discoveryprogram@montclair.edu , or visit us at  montclair.edu/discovery-program .

title: Major & Career Exploration Talks: What We Wish We Knew Before Declaring!
description:
Not sure what major to choose or where your career path is headed? You’re not alone! Join us for  Major & Career Exploration Talks: What We Wish We Knew Before Declaring! , a dynamic panel event where upperclassmen students share their real, unfiltered experiences about choosing their majors and navigating their career journeys. Hear firsthand what they wish they knew before declaring, the challenges they faced, and how they found their passions. Whether you're feeling undecided, overwhelmed, or just curious about different paths, this event will give you insights, inspiration, and practical advice to help you make informed decisions. Don’t miss this opportunity to gain valuable perspectives and take the next step in your academic and career journey! * Food and refreshments will be served! 
 This workshop is presented by  The Discovery Program . To contact the Discovery Program, email us at  discoveryprogram@montclair.edu , or visit us at  montclair.edu/discovery-program .

title: Transgender Awareness Week Flag Raising & Gathering
description:
Join Advocacy & Outreach in the Office of Student Belonging at our Trans Awareness Week flag raising to commemorate the start of Trans Awareness Week. 
 We will receive words from some of our students 
 The flag raising is at the Student Center Flag poles from 2:00pm - 2:30pm 
 The gathering after is at the Advocacy & Outreach Office, Suite 113 of the Student Center from 2:30pm - 3:30pm 
 All are welcome!

title: Open Mic Night
description: Whether you’ve got a hidden talent or just want a fun night out, Open Mic Night is the place to be. Music, poetry, comedy, and good vibes all around—come perform, cheer on your friends, or just hang out and enjoy the energy. 🎤

title: Turtle Back Zoo
description:
Join your friends for a day at the Zoo! See animals from all over the world as you enjoy lunch at your choice of cafes around the park. Bring cash for lunch, a water bottle to stay hydrated, and comfortable walking shoes. 
   
 How do I sign-up? 
 
 Register online through Fusion, in the Student Recreation Center's main office, or over the phone (973-655-3340) . 
 Cost for Students  $20 
 
   
 Where and when? 
 
 November 21 2026  
 10am-4pm 
 Begins at Student Recreation Center 
 
   
 What to bring:  Water, lunch, long pants, sneakers, and sunscreen.   Participants MUST wear sneakers in order to go on the zipline and obstacle course. 
   
 Note:  Tree Top Adventures restricts obstacle course and zipline participants to a strict weight limit of 250 lbs. Students may opt to participate in only the Zoo portion of the trip should they like. 
   
 Potential Hazards:  Unpredictable weather changes, sunburn/heat exhaustion, dehydration, allergic reactions/poisonous plants, insect bites and injury by running, including sprains, lacerations, abrasions and fractures.

title: Women's History Month Flag Raising & Reception
description:
Attention Montclair + Bloomfield Communities! 
   
 Get ready to raise the flag at the A&O annual Women's History Month flag raising and reception! 
 Join us Monday, March 1, 2027 to come together and honor the incredible achievements and ongoing impact of the women who make our campus community and world at large shine bright. 
 We'll begin with a special  flag raising ceremony at 12:30 PM in the Student Center Flag Pole  to collectively lift up the trailblazing women who have paved the way for us all. 
   
 Then, head over to the  Student Center Ballroom A&B  from 1:00 - 2:00 PM for a reception  you won't want to miss. We'll be mixing, mingling, eating, partying, crafting, and highlighting the talent, passion, and brilliance of the inspiring women in our midst. 
 This is your chance to celebrate, connect, and show some serious school spirit. Bring your energy, bring your pride, and get ready for an unforgettable afternoon! 
   
 Tell a friend to tell a friend. We can't wait to see you there!

title: Denim Day: Mind the Gaps, Move Forward
description:
April is Sexual Assault Awareness Month, and together, we can break the silence.  The Montclair State University Police Department and the Office of Advocacy & Outreach are committed to fostering a campus where survivors feel heard, supported, and empowered.  It starts with believing. It starts with you.  By offering a safe and affirming response to those who disclose gender-based and sexual violence, we can create lasting change. Let’s stand together to end the cycle of silence—because  every voice matters, and every action counts. 
 Denim Day is recognized internationally as a day of action and awareness, encouraging people to wear denim to challenge victim-blaming and educate others about sexual violence.  This year, Denim Day falls on Wednesday, April 28, 2027. 
 The University Police and  Office of Advocacy & Outreach and the Office of Advocacy and Community Resilience , in partnership with over 30 campus and local community organizations, invites you to join us  from 9 a.m. to 12 p.m. at the Amphitheater  for a powerful awareness event honoring survivors and increasing awareness of available resources. 
 Despite the  gaps  that still exist in the criminal justice system, in resources, and in prevention education,  we will move forward together  as a community. As part of this commitment, we will walk  one mile around campus in solidarity with survivors.
